sections.label: THINKING
sections.content:
Now I understand the mechanic better:
- The green container at top (node 2, N color) shrank from 14 to 5 pixels after clicking on the light green shape inside the container
- The green container still has the red dot (node 4) inside it
- The container seems to be shrinking towards just the red dot
The pattern seems to be: clicking on a colored shape inside a container causes the matching-colored object elsewhere on the board to shrink. The goal might be to shrink all the colored objects completely (or to just the red dots).
Let me now try clicking on the other light green shape (node 14) to see if it also affects the green container, or if there's a different relationship.
Actually wait - looking at the ascii output more carefully:
- Row 9: 'BBGNNBBBBB' - the green container is now just 2 N pixels wide instead of the original 5
- The G (dark gray) border is still there on the left side
So the N (light green) pixels are being removed. The container is shrinking. Let me try clicking on the other N shape to see what happens.

sections.label: TOOL CALL: python
sections.content:
<tool_call>
<function=python>
<parameter=code>
# Try clicking on the other light green shape (node 14) in the middle-right container
# Node 14 boundary: [[20, 44], [20, 46], [22, 46], [22, 44], [22, 45], [21, 46], [20, 45]]
# Center roughly at (21, 45)
action([{'action': 'MOUSE', 'row': 21, 'col': 45}])
</parameter>
</function>
</tool_call>
